articles.title: Severe heatwave hits AP as temp nears 48 degrees C - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
Piduguralla in Palnadu district recorded the state’s highest temperature at 47.6 degrees centigrade, the highest recorded so far this summer. Kankipadu in Krishna district and Nandanamarella in Markapuram district registered 46.1 degrees centigrade each, while Tanuku in West Godavari district touched 46 degrees centigrade. Temperatures above 45 degrees centigrade were reported across 11 districts.
The heatwave has intensified to the extent that even people remaining indoors are reportedly suffering heat-related illnesses. Authorities said daytime conditions are becoming increasingly dangerous, particularly for the elderly, children, pregnant women and outdoor workers.
APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain said the state is witnessing a rapid escalation in heat intensity, with temperatures above 43 degrees centigrade recorded in 204 mandals spread across 18 districts on Wednesday alone. Palnadu district accounted for 23 such mandals, followed by Krishna with 21, Prakasam and Eluru with 20 each, and Bapatla with 17.
The disaster management authority warned that severe heatwave conditions would continue over the next several days. On Thursday alone, temperatures between 45 and 47 degrees centigrade are forecast in districts including East Godavari, West Godavari, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Bapatla, Palnadu and Prakasam.
Officials said 74 mandals are likely to experience severe heatwave conditions on Thursday, while another 232 mandals are expected to remain under heatwave conditions. On Friday, severe heatwave alerts remain in force for 24 mandals, with heatwave conditions forecast in 270 mandals.
Authorities have advised people to avoid outdoor movement between 11 am and 4 pm unless absolutely necessary. Medical experts recommend continuous hydration through water, buttermilk and coconut water, while also urging residents to use head coverings and avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight.
